skill.name: vercel-react-view-transitions
skill.description: Guide for adding React View Transition API animations to React and Next.js applications. It covers route transitions, shared elements, Suspense reveals, list reorders, transition types, and reusable CSS recipes.
skill.summary: Add native React view transitions for routes, shared elements, Suspense reveals, and list changes.
skill.author: vercel-labs

security_audit.summary: The static analyzer reported many external command, weak cryptography, keylogger, reconnaissance, and network indicators. Review found these are documentation and code-example false positives from Markdown backticks, React keys, CSS/animation terms, and reference links; no executable skill code, malware behavior, prompt injection, or data exfiltration was found. The remaining risk is low because the README includes a user-run install command and the metadata/README include public documentation URLs.

low_findings.title: Static Analyzer False Positives in Markdown Examples
low_findings.description: The reported Ruby backtick execution, weak cryptography, keylogger, and reconnaissance indicators are caused by Markdown inline code and React terminology such as key, name, transition types, and route examples. These files provide implementation guidance and do not define executable scripts that run shell commands or collect input.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged content is inside Markdown documentation and JSX/TSX examples, not runnable skill code. Targeted review found no shell execution, credential access, keylogging logic, or prompt injection language.

low_findings.title: Benign External References and User-Run Commands
low_findings.description: The skill includes public documentation links and an installation command for users to run manually. These are expected for a documentation skill, but they are still external references that users should inspect before using.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URLs point to React, Next.js, GitHub, and skills.sh references, and the command is shown as installation documentation. There is no evidence that the skill executes the command automatically.

content.value_statement: React and Next.js apps often need polished transitions without adding a large animation stack. This skill gives agents a structured workflow for native view transitions, shared elements, Suspense reveals, and safe CSS recipes.
actual_capabilities: Audits React routes, links, Suspense boundaries, and persistent layout elements before implementation.
Adds page-level view transitions with transition types for forward and back navigation.
Guides shared element transitions with unique names and fallback animation behavior.
Provides complete CSS recipes for fade, slide, morph, scale, persistent elements, and reduced motion.
Explains Next.js App Router setup, link transition types, loading files, and Server Component usage.
Documents common mistakes such as layout-level transitions and missing default none settings.
limitations: Requires React canary features outside Next.js environments that already bundle the needed React version.
Native view transitions depend on browser support and degrade without animation in unsupported browsers.
The skill provides guidance and examples, but it does not inspect a running browser by itself.
Complex visual correctness still needs manual testing across navigation paths and reduced motion settings.

use_cases.title: Add route transitions to a product app
use_cases.description: Map list-to-detail navigation and add forward and back transitions without changing the app architecture.

use_cases.title: Improve loading states in a Next.js app
use_cases.description: Animate skeleton-to-content reveals while keeping persistent controls stable during Suspense resolution.

use_cases.title: Standardize animation implementation reviews
use_cases.description: Use the checklist to verify shared element names, reduced motion CSS, and transition placement decisions.

prompt_templates.title: Audit my app for view transitions
prompt_templates.prompt: Audit this React app for view transition opportunities. Map the routes, links, Suspense boundaries, persistent elements, and shared visual elements before making changes.

prompt_templates.title: Add basic page transitions
prompt_templates.prompt: Add React ViewTransition page animations for hierarchical navigation in this app. Use transition types, default none settings, and the provided CSS recipes.

prompt_templates.title: Add shared element morphs
prompt_templates.prompt: Implement shared element transitions between the grid and detail pages. Use globally unique names, preserve list identity, and document fallback behavior.

prompt_templates.title: Integrate with Next.js App Router
prompt_templates.prompt: Apply the Next.js view transition workflow to this App Router project. Configure the experimental flag if needed, handle Link transition types, and animate loading.tsx reveals.

output_examples.input: A gallery app has a grid page, detail page, and image thumbnails shared between both views.
output_examples.output: The agent returns a navigation map, wraps the page components, assigns unique shared element names to images, and adds reduced motion CSS.

output_examples.input: A dashboard has Suspense-loaded tables and a persistent sidebar that should not slide during page changes.
output_examples.output: The agent isolates the sidebar, adds reveal transitions to loading states, and keeps page navigation animations separate from data refreshes.

output_examples.input: A Next.js app uses App Router links and server-rendered filtering through URL parameters.
output_examples.output: The agent recommends the view transition config, transition types on links when available, and startTransition for programmatic filter updates.

best_practices: Start with a navigation audit before adding any transition wrappers.
Use default none and explicitly enable only the intended transition triggers.
Test every navigation path with shared elements, Suspense reveals, and reduced motion enabled.
anti_patterns: Do not add layout-level transitions around pages that need page-level enter and exit behavior.
Do not use router.back for animated back navigation because it bypasses transition typing.
Do not reuse the same shared element name in multiple mounted components.

faq.question: Does this skill install React canary?
faq.answer: No. It explains when React canary is needed and notes that Next.js App Router bundles the needed React support.

faq.question: Can it animate shared images between pages?
faq.answer: Yes. It explains how to use matching ViewTransition names and unique identifiers for shared visual elements.

faq.question: Does it support Next.js?
faq.answer: Yes. It covers the App Router, the viewTransition config, transitionTypes on links, loading files, and Server Components.

faq.question: Will unsupported browsers break the app?
faq.answer: No. Native view transitions degrade gracefully, so unsupported browsers skip the animation behavior.

faq.question: Does it include accessibility guidance?
faq.answer: Yes. It requires reduced motion CSS and explains how to keep persistent controls stable.

faq.question: Can it replace all animation libraries?
faq.answer: No. It focuses on native view transitions between UI states, not every animation need in a product.
